segments.text: This is what the Lord God showed me. I saw that he was preparing a swarm of locusts just when the spring crops began to grow. (Spring crops begin to grow right after the time when the king's hay is cut.)
And so when the locusts finished eating every green plant in the fields, I pleaded with the Lord God,
Please forgive your people! How can the descendants of Jacob survive? They are so weak.
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: So the Lord changed his mind.
It won't happen,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: said the Lord.
This is what the Lord God showed me. I saw that the Lord God was calling for a judgment of fire. The fire burned up the depths of the sea and destroyed the farmland.
I pleaded with the Lord God,
Please stop! How can the descendants of Jacob survive? They are so weak.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So the Lord changed his mind.
This too won't happen,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: said the Lord.
This is what he showed me. I saw the Lord was standing beside a wall that had been built with a plumb line.  He was holding a plumb line in his hand.
And the Lord asked me,
Amos, what do you see?
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: I replied,
A plumb line.
segments.speaker: the Lord God

segments.text: And the Lord God said,
I am placing a plumb line in the midst of my people Israel.  I won't ignore their sins anymore.
segments.speaker: the Lord God

segments.text: The high places of the descendants of Isaac will be torn down, and the holy places of Israel will be destroyed. Sword in hand, I will rise up against the house of Jeroboam.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Amaziah, priest of Bethel, sent a message to Jeroboam, king of Israel, saying,
Amos is plotting against you among the people of Israel. What he's saying is unbearable!
segments.speaker: the Lord God

segments.text: For he's saying Jeroboam will be killed by the sword, and the people will be taken away from their land into exile.
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: Amaziah said to Amos,
Go away prophet! Run away to the land of Judah. Go and prophesy there to earn your bread.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: But don't ever prophesy again at Bethel, for this is where the king worships, the national Temple.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: But Amos replied,
I'm not a trained prophet,  or the son of a prophet. I was just a shepherd, and also took care of fig trees.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: The Lord took me from following my flock, and the Lord told me, ‘Go, and give my message to my people of Israel.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: So now hear what the Lord is telling you: You say,
Don't prophesy against Israel, and don't preach against the descendants of Isaac.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: But this is what the Lord says: Your wife will become a prostitute in the city; your sons and daughters will be killed by the sword. Your land will be measured and divided up, and you yourself will die in a foreign land. The people of Israel will definitely be taken from the land and go into exile.
